verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I TM protein with documented dual localization to ER-Golgi compartments and the plasma membrane. It cycles through the cell surface as part of normal vesicular cargo trafficking, including transport of GPI-anchored proteins and other cargoes. While the dominant steady-state localization is ER-Golgi endomembrane system, the protein has its own TM anchor and actively reaches the outer face of the PM via its luminal domain-driven trafficking. This constitutes a documented but non-baseline PM pool that qualifies as contextual rather than canonical surface residence (verdict yes), as the bulk of TMED10 resides intracellularly and the surface pool is transient cycling rather than stable surface anchoring.
